items.title: Massachusetts’ life sciences employment dipped last year following 14 years of growth: report
items.snippet: Massachusetts’ life sciences jobs declined 1% in 2025, according to a new MassBioEd report. However, the report is projecting industry employment will increase 9.7% by 2030. It also noted reskilling needs for some roles, including scientists.

items.title: Lilly and Boehringer roll back billions in German investments over health budget cuts
items.snippet: With Germany moving to curb healthcare spending, Eli Lilly and Boehringer Ingelheim have rethought plans to invest in facilities, including a manufacturing plant for GLP-1 drugs.

items.title: Peptide biotech Parabilis targets up to $476M IPO
items.snippet: Parabilis Medicines is joining the parade of biotechs going public, sharing plans to raise as much as $476.4 million to help advance its lead cancer candidate into late-stage testing.

items.title: ADC Therapeutics nosedives on 27 deaths in confirmatory trial for lymphoma drug
items.snippet: In addition to a high rate of deaths, ADC Therapeutics’ Zynlonta plus rituximab showed no overall survival benefit in patients with diffuse large B cell lymphoma, casting doubt on its value as a second-line treatment in this indication.

items.title: Regeneron deepens relationship with CytomX, adding up to $2B and eight targets
items.snippet: All told, CytomX Therapeutics now stands to receive up to $4 billion over the course of its partnership with Regeneron, if all milestones are met.

items.title: Alnylam hops on AI train with up to $2B Inceptive partnership
items.snippet: Alnylam Pharmaceuticals will leverage Inceptive Nucleics’ generative machine learning models to accelerate the development of RNA interference therapies.

items.title: Rethinking hair loss treatment
items.snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you'll be hearing from Daniel Gil, CEO of Pelage Pharmaceuticals and Francisco Ramírez-Valle, senior vice president of immunology discovery at Eli Lilly. We dive into the long-overlooked hair loss space, exploring why true innovation has lagged, how a regenerative approach aims to reactiv...

items.title: How long is too long? Weighing and accounting for employment gaps
items.snippet: It’s not unusual for it to take unemployed biopharma professionals awhile to land new jobs. Three experts discuss when employment gaps are an issue and how to explain them to prospective employers.

items.title: The top 12 companies hiring in biopharma now
items.snippet: Looking for a biopharma job? Check out the BioSpace list of 12 top companies hiring life sciences professionals like you.

items.title: FDA hits Medline with warning letter over long-running contamination problem
items.snippet: FDA inspectors found that Medline, which raised billions of dollars last year, failed to prevent repeat bacterial contamination of finished drug products.

items.title: ASCO’s biggest winners showcased a new era of cancer innovation
items.snippet: Revolution Medicines stole the show at the 2026 American Society of Clinical Oncology meeting as full data from its pancreatic cancer drug lived up to expectations, while Summit and Akeso proved the PD-(L)1/VEGF mechanism and Eli Lilly showed that its in vivo CAR T bet is paying off.

items.title: NewLimit snags $435M after seeing age reversal in human liver cells
items.snippet: NewLimit is pressing the gas, speeding into clinical trials much sooner than expected after lab research showed its epigenetic reprogramming asset reversed aging in human liver cells.

items.title: Celcuity slides even after ‘practice-changing’ late-stage breast cancer data
items.snippet: Celcuity’s gedatolisib doubled progression-free survival versus standard of care in certain patients with advanced breast cancer. Still, the biotech’s stock dropped more than 25% Tuesday.

items.title: Travere beefs up rare kidney disease portfolio with up to $1.1B China deal
items.snippet: Travere Therapeutics will gain an exclusive license to the oral BTK inhibitor civorebrutinib, which analysts at Guggenheim Securities said could be “complementary” to the biotech’s IgA nephropathy drug Filspari.

items.title: FDA taps prior knowledge to expedite cell and gene therapy development
items.snippet: A year of significant policy change at the FDA brought momentum and scrutiny into the new year. As 2026 gets underway, biopharma companies are responding to sweeping vaccine changes while concerns surface about the politicization of the agency.

items.title: Legend soars on ‘best-in-class,’ ‘best-in-disease’ in vivo CAR T in early lymphoma study
items.snippet: All six non-Hodgkin lymphoma patients on Legend Biotech’s CAR T therapy responded to treatment—findings that could make the biotech an attractive takeover target, according to analysts at Oppenheimer.

items.title: Lilly targets kidney disease with Ascidian’s RNA exon editors for up to $1.9B
items.snippet: For an investment of up to $1.9 billion, Eli Lilly will be able to use Ascidian Therapeutics’ platform that removes mutated exons from mRNA molecules, avoiding the expression of disease-causing proteins.

items.title: ASCO wins from RevMed, Akeso/Summit, more; plus more Lilly and more China; ADA on deck
items.snippet: Improved survival on display at the American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) in Chicago; Pfizer’s unusual pact with China’s Innovent highlights a new type of collaboration; Eli Lilly continues its nonstop deal streak, including with Chinese biotechs; and looking ahead to this weekend’s American Diabetes Associatio...

items.title: Facing pressure from China, BioSpace’s NextGen biotech leaders say: ‘Bring it on’
items.snippet: From Chinese innovation to AI, biotech CEOs are being hit with challenges at a breakneck pace. Three leaders from BioSpace’s NextGen Class of 2026 told us about the issues keeping them up at night.

items.title: No longer a bargain pool, Chinese biotechs command higher premiums
items.snippet: Deal dynamics between Chinese biotechs and global pharma companies are changing fast, with the biotechs seeking higher upfront payments and the Big Pharmas seeking more expansive platform deals.

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
items.snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.
